initiative.name: OpenIncident v0.5 - Escalation Policies & Alert Lifecycle
initiative.description: Implement multi-tier escalation policies that automatically notify on-call users and escalate to backup responders when alerts are unacknowledged. Users can define escalation chains with timeout rules, fallback responders, and repeat cycles. This completes the core alerting → on-call → escalation flow, matching PagerDuty's core functionality while remaining open-source and self-hosted.
success_criteria: When a critical alert arrives and is unacknowledged, it escalates through defined tiers with configurable timeouts
Escalation policies can target schedules, specific users, or both in multi-tier chains
Escalation state is tracked per alert with full audit trail (who was notified when, who acknowledged)
Escalation UI visualizes the policy flow: Tier 1 (oncall-primary, 5min) → Tier 2 (oncall-backup, 10min) → Repeat
PagerDuty schedule import tool migrates schedules and policies to OpenIncident with validation report
Alerts routed via escalation policies trigger Slack DMs and track acknowledgment state
initiative.architecture_notes: Escalation evaluation is event-driven: alert fires → trigger escalation → background worker polls escalation state → fires notifications at tier timeouts → marks acknowledged when user responds. EscalationPolicy has ordered tiers with targets (schedule_id or user_ids) and timeout_seconds. EscalationState tracks current_tier and last_notified_at per alert. Worker runs every 30s checking for timed-out tiers. Acknowledgment can come from Slack interactive button, API call, or CLI command. No complex queueing needed — polling is sufficient at expected scale (<10k alerts/day).
